title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE TAGASAKA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ“ BIGA-AN ANNEX IN BARANGAY BIGA-AN, MUNICIPALITY OF HINATUAN, PROVINCE OF SURIGAO DEL SUR FROM THE TAGASAKA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL. CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS BIGA-AN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
109 OG No. 4, 400 (January 28, 2013)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10263, November 15, 2012 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE TAGASAKA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ“ BIGA-AN ANNEX IN BARANGAY BIGA-AN, MUNICIPALITY OF HINATUAN, PROVINCE OF SURIGAO DEL SUR FROM THE TAGASAKA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL. CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS BIGA-AN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into an Independent National High School.

ГўВЂВ“ The Tagasaka National High School ГўВЂВ“ Biga-an Annex in Barangay Biga-an, Municipality of Hinatuan, Province of Surigao del Sur is hereby separated from the Tagasaka National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as Biga-an National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

ГўВЂВ“ All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Tagasaka National High School ГўВЂВ“ Biga-an Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the Biga-an National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations.

ГўВЂВ“ The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the DepartmentГўВЂВ™s program the operationalization of the Biga-an National High School the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current yearГўВЂВ™s appropriation of the Tagasaka National High School ГўВЂВ“ Biga-an Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation. ГўВЂВ“

The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations that may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

ГўВЂВ“ This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on August 3, 2011 and July 24, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

NOV 15 2012

(Sgd.) BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
